transcript.text: Okay.
Do I need to be worried?
No, sorry.
No, that's just from running around and like, but no, you absolutely don't need to be worried.
It's all positive.
Just meaning there's an, if the, I don't know if the note takers send to whoever's invited to calls.
So if ever like a different client, I tend to just not mention it, but it was all positive.
Yeah.
Okay.
Makes sense.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Big fans.
I don't know if this is a Mac thing, but I, you know, in the Google meet the little windows that are with everybody's heads.
My head is getting cut off.
Oh, this used to not happen on PC.
So I don't know if people are seeing like half my face or something or.
Oh,
So jarring for you.
No, it's, it looks normal for me.
Is it like, do you have, let me just see, I honestly am not as good at Google Meet yet, but I know you can choose different, like, views.
Yeah, yeah.
And the dots, I think if you go to change layout, I don't know if that'll actually help or not, but.
Mm-hmm.
I tried that, but yeah, it's weird.
It's like the preview shows my entire screen, but then when I enter, it's like zoomed in on just my face.
And if I move, say, if I move here, I'm out of the window.
Weird.
Like, I can't see myself.
So weird.
Is it like, is your browser window like pretty small or is it expanded to like, because sometimes that happens.
Oh, okay.
I don't know.
Yeah.
Weird, weird.
don't know.
Yeah.
I don't know why people love the maximum.
Honestly, I think it's an American thing.
Yeah, yeah, you're probably right.
I haven't had, I know we've talked about this, but I haven't had experience with a PC in like a few years.
So it's one of those things where like you kind of don't know what you're missing in a way.
Like this is kind of all I know how to do, especially with the iPhone.
It's just sort of like all in that same ecosystem.
So I wonder if I did go back to a PC if I'd be like, oh, this is so much better, which is always an option.
Yeah, yeah.
I don't know if it's better, but I think I think Androids are definitely better than iPhone.
Yeah, yeah.
Hey, Vanessa, how are you?
Guys, how's it going?
Doing well.
How about yourself?
Good.
Good.
Can't complain.
Awesome.
Yeah.
Well, I know.
Oh, yeah.
Sorry.
But I know obviously we saw Ian's message.
Thank you for keeping us up to date.
would love to just hear.
you for
From you, kind of how you're thinking about it, any context or feedback you're willing to share, and then we can go from there.
Yeah, sure.
So short and sweet answer is that we hired a full time person who's going to be our developer relations staff.
And so her job will be to work on all the content, write content to, you know, conference coordination, stuff like that.
So basically, we, you know, we've hired full time staff.
And so we just don't really have a need right now to augment with GrowthX since we've got running our team.
Totally.
Okay, that makes perfect sense.
Yeah, that's good to know.
Okay, so maybe we chat through some of the open articles and where we'd like to get those.
We did hire a cybersecurity expert.
Hopefully, there will be a little bit of an uptick in quality for some of the articles.
Also, it sounds like the person you've hired in-house will probably take over most of the responsibilities.
And this is purely like if it ends up being useful to you guys, we are building out more of the specialist network.
So I think some of the issues we had in the past with the content not feeling like it appropriately matched the audience should go away.

So if there is ever a point where you would like to outsource SEO and have the in-house person focus on something else, or you'd like us to focus on, you know, another asset that's always available to you.
So anyway, I just wanted to address that.
I feel like there was a gap in quality and tone.
And I just, anyway, just wanted you to know that we were working towards addressing that.
And that if there is ever an opportunity in the future to work together, ideally that...
There'll be a smaller gap and we'll be a little bit more in step.
Yeah, awesome.
That sounds great.
Super good to know.
Yeah, I know I'll have our Deborah kind of take a look at the articles and see, you know, what she thinks about them.
But great to know that you guys are kind of starting to build up, build out that network and work on that.
So that's great.
Yeah.
Also, if there's any other feedback, like, you know, obviously the feedback that we get from the people that we work with is, you know, kind of gold for us.
It helps us get better.
So if there's anything else that you would want to share about your experience that could help us improve, we'd be so open to hearing it and thankful for that feedback.
Don't want to put you on the spot or anything.
And if you, you know, think of something later on, feel free to ping me.
But whatever you have to share is useful to us and we appreciate it.
Yeah, I mean, you know, you guys have been a pleasure to work with.
So I think, you know, on the customer success.
On and the delivery side, you guys have been awesome.
We really appreciate your organization, your communication, your friendliness, and you guys have been really wonderful as partners for us, so we're super appreciative of that.
On the content side, I think we just have a really niche market in terms of what we're trying to deliver.
Unfortunately, a lot of the folks that we're building our content for are very aware of what's generated by AI.

So, you know, it's hard to narrow in on that, but we appreciate all the work you guys have done, and I think we really appreciate the feedback you've taken and how you've worked to improve the product and the content of our partnership.
So, yeah, there's really not much more I have to say on that.
Just thanks for all your support.
Yeah, well, thank you again for
Or for like walking us through it.
It's very valuable.
I'm happy to hear that.
I'm also, I wish we'd gotten, we could have closed that gap sooner because we love working with you guys.
But also it sounds like maybe there's a need for someone in-house and it makes sense for a super specialized topic and we're excited for you guys to get exactly what you need.
But thank you for the kind words.
We obviously, we've loved working with you.
So sorry to see you go.
And maybe at some point in the future, there will be an opportunity to work together again and would jump at the chance, but totally understand.
And yeah, so we'll do what we need to do to get everything in a good place for you guys.
I will also loop you in.
Would you be the correct point of contact to loop in with like our finance team to deal with the sort of like the last invoicing or sort of the finer details of ending the engagement?
So Ian is going to be the best point of contact for that.
Once we, you know, once you guys figure that out, just feel free to loop him in.
His schedule is a little bit crazy this week, but
I'm sure he will let him know that, you know, he should expect something from you guys and we'll coordinate from there.
Okay, cool.
Sounds good.

Any questions that you have on the content that's currently in your court to review or anything like any priorities we should be aware of just so that we leave everything like the absolute best place for you or for this new person coming on?
No, I don't think so.
know, it looks like, yeah, there's 14 articles for review.
Yeah, I think this is great and I will take a look at it or have our new DevRel take a look at it and then we'll go from there.
Okay, great.
All right.
Well, thank you so much again.
And if anything else comes up, please feel free to ping us.
We'll hop on a call whenever.
And we've loved working with you.
So we'll keep the syncs going until the end of the engagement.
And then if at any point you're like, hey, we can do this async, we're flexible to whatever you're.
So when exactly is the end of the engagement?
That will be up to the finance team.
They'll probably look at the contract and see what the what their sort of details are.
And then maybe, you know, they'll connect with Ian and chat through what makes sense.
Yeah.
So it depends on what's in that SOW and the contract itself.
So I can't say for sure, actually, right now.
But once they connect, determine what that end date is, we can kind of work backwards from there.
OK, that sounds great.
All right.
All right.
Well, thanks.
Have a wonderful rest of your day.
Thank you so much for all of your help so far.
And yeah, we appreciate it working with you guys.
Yeah, you too.
All right.
We'll chat with you.
Have a good day.
Bye.
Thanks.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss the end of GrowthX's engagement with Promptfoo and address final steps.](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=1.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Promptfoo hired a full-time developer relations staff, eliminating the need for GrowthX's services](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)
  - [GrowthX has improved their specialist network to address previous quality/tone issues](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)
  - [Both parties expressed mutual appreciation for the partnership](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
  - [Finance teams will coordinate the details of ending the engagement](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)

## Topics

### Promptfoo's Decision to End Engagement

  - [Hired full-time developer relations staff to handle content, conference coordination, etc.](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)
  - [New hire eliminates current need for GrowthX's services](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)
  - [Promptfoo has a niche market, making it challenging for external content creation](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=377.0)

### GrowthX's Service Improvements

  - [Hired a cybersecurity expert to improve article quality](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=234.0)
  - [Building out specialist network to better match audience needs](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
  - [Addressing previous gaps in quality and tone](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)
  - [Open to future collaboration if Promptfoo needs to outsource SEO or other assets](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=268.0)

### Feedback on GrowthX's Performance

  - [Positive feedback on customer success and delivery](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=356.0)
  - [Praised for organization, communication, and friendliness](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Appreciated GrowthX's efforts to improve based on feedback](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=394.0)
  - [Challenge: Promptfoo's audience is highly aware of AI-generated content](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)

### Current Content Status

  - [14 articles pending review by Promptfoo](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
  - [New DevRel staff will likely review the content](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)

### Engagement Wrap-up Details

  - [Ian (Promptfoo) will be the point of contact for finance-related matters](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)
  - [Exact end date of engagement to be determined by finance teams and contract details](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=544.0)
  - [GrowthX will continue syncs until the end of engagement, flexible to async communication if preferred](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=534.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Promptfoo's new DevRel to review pending articles](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [GrowthX's finance team to connect with Ian regarding final invoicing and engagement end date](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [Continue scheduled syncs until the official end of engagement](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [GrowthX to ensure all deliverables are in the best possible state for handover](https://fathom.video/share/Vx2qGWQekf-s8iAXFyzLTeGzxjie_s8E?tab=summary&timestamp=453.0)

action_items.description: Loop in finance team re final invoicing details. Connect w/ Ian (Promptfoo) once sorted.
Review/have new DevRel review 14 pending articles. Provide feedback.
Continue weekly syncs until engagement end. Adjust to async if requested by Promptfoo.
